channel.title: Teacher Plus
channel.description: The magazine for the contemporary teacher

item.title: Displaced and devastated: indigenous communities
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Deepika Gupta

Learn about the unique characteristics that define indigenous communities, their historical connections, and their struggles to maintain their cultural heritage.
Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Deepika Gupta

Learn about the unique characteristics that define indigenous communities, their historical connections, and their struggles to maintain their cultural heritage.

item.title: Math challenges for curious learners
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.content:encoded: Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Nirmala Raman
Mathematical concepts aren’t just for sums; they also come handy in daily life. For example, geometry can be useful in martial arts. Nirmala Raman’s worksheets will help ease many such mathematical challenges for young and curious learners.

item.title: What buddies do together
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.content:encoded: Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Anumeha Fatehpuria
How do children choose their friends? How do they tell a bully from a friend? Anumeha Fatehpuria’s worksheet this month will help children assess their social lives at school.

item.title: Engage with English
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.content:encoded: Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Gita Krenek
Our worksheet this month aims to help 4th and 5th graders to understand and practice grammar rules.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: im not able to access the magazine whereas i have paid two days for the whole year

item.title: Festive mathematics
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.content:encoded: Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Nirmala Raman
Nirmala Raman’s worksheet shows how various Indian festive traditions can teach division, multiplication, fractions, and logical thinking in enjoyable ways.

item.title: Food: beyond the classroom plate
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.excerpt:encoded:
Mahak Khanna
How does food travel from farm to lunchbox? Is your lunchbox keeping you alert in class? Mahak Khanna’s worksheet this month delves into the ecosystem of food and energy.

item.title: Discover, observe, explore!
item.dc:creator: Kumar
item.content:encoded: Please click here for the worksheet
item.excerpt:encoded:
Geetha Iyer
From lentils to living things, ask questions and seek answers in this exciting worksheet that gets children to observe, analyze, and create, as they learn to classify, repurpose things we throw away as waste, identify fingerprints, prepare balanced diets, reason and make connections, and use a microscope. This worksheet takes science from their books and makes it very real for children.
